name: run_verification_suite
description: Run lint, typecheck, tests, and build commands after code changes.
examples: Run python -m unittest discover -s tests

name: generate_handoff_packet
description: Create a structured handoff packet when context usage reaches rollover threshold.
examples: Generate handoff with reason context_rollover

name: preview_regression_check
description: Capture and compare preview snapshots for UI tasks.
examples: Compare preview before and after login form update

name: workspace_search
description: Search code and docs quickly to ground planning in project evidence.
examples: Search for TODO|FIXME in src and docs

name: generate_public_report
description: Create a concise run report and social thread draft from session artifacts.
examples: Generate report for latest session

name: agent_provider_conformance
description: Keep Codex and MiniMax provider profiles, model defaults, and plan-first boot behavior aligned with official docs and honest runtime semantics.
examples: Align the Codex provider defaults with current OpenAI Codex model docs
Document the MiniMax custom model_provider profile and preserve-think rules

name: fluxio_workbench_studio
description: Design Fluxio's operator UI, skill library, workflow studio, and Git-aware workspace surfaces around real user tasks.
examples: Design the workspace panel to show branch, dirty state, and safe deploy actions
Refine the skill studio for non-technical and expert users

name: browser_use_local_inspection
description: Use the Browser Use local-browser workflow for localhost, file, and in-app browser inspection with screenshots and interaction proof.
examples: Open the local desktop preview and verify the model selector drawer
Capture before and after screenshots for a UI regression

name: leon_lin_design_taste
description: Package the installed high-taste design guidance into Fluxio: strong hierarchy, anti-generic layouts, expressive typography, and production-grade UI polish.
examples: Redesign the model selector without generic AI gradients
Audit the drawer UI for hierarchy, spacing, and state clarity

name: high_end_visual_design
description: Apply agency-grade visual design: nested surfaces, calibrated spacing, premium motion, clear state hierarchy, and non-generic component structure.
examples: Upgrade controls to nested, haptic selector islands
Replace flat cards with intentional surface hierarchy

name: gpt_taste_frontend_motion
description: Use strict frontend taste rules for bold layout variance, cleaner typography, dense bento logic, and motion that avoids default AI-looking UI.
examples: Improve a selector drawer with richer hover and active states
Audit a bento dashboard for empty cells and weak hierarchy

name: frontend_image_direction
description: Generate or interpret premium website image references, then translate the visual direction into implementable frontend layout and styling.
examples: Use a screenshot as visual direction for a drawer redesign
Create a compact UI mockup direction before implementing CSS

name: industrial_brutalist_ui
description: Apply raw mechanical UI direction for analyst/operator surfaces: Swiss grids, dense evidence, sharp divisions, and terminal-grade clarity.
examples: Make a runtime status panel feel more like an operator console
Refine dense controls without losing readability

name: minimalist_editorial_ui
description: Use restrained editorial UI rules for warm monochrome surfaces, typographic contrast, flat bento layouts, and calm premium product screens.
examples: Simplify noisy settings controls
Give a model selector a calmer editorial hierarchy

name: ui_refactor_expert
description: Refactor user interfaces for clarity, accessibility, responsive behavior, state quality, and consistency while preserving existing app behavior.
examples: Improve the Skills Hub so a non-technical user can understand active skills
Refactor the agent composer for keyboard and screen-reader accessibility

name: frontend_taste_director
description: Review frontend work for visual hierarchy, spacing rhythm, interaction fidelity, mobile behavior, and non-generic app polish before implementation.
examples: Review a new skill studio flow before broad component edits
Call out visual risks before changing the runtime workbench

name: jbheaven_godmode_lab
description: Use the imported NAS Hermes Godmode/G0DM0D3 skill bundle for authorized JBHEAVEN, GBEH, Gandalf, and controlled red-team lab work with transcript proof, Hermes/OpenClaw routing, MiniMax-capable model selection, and safety-scoped model probing.
examples: Run a safe JBHEAVEN red-team proof through Hermes or OpenClaw and save the transcript
Package the NAS Hermes godmode skill and related helpers into a reviewable artifact

name: hermes_skill_packager
description: Select, package, and document the strongest imported NAS Hermes skills into portable zip bundles with manifests, provenance, validation notes, and app-catalog mapping.
examples: Create a JBHEAVEN skill zip from godmode, obliteratus, Hermes agent, OpenClaw routing, and frontend helper skills
Write a manifest that explains which NAS skills were selected and why

name: runtime_loop_supervisor
description: Design and verify self-running agent loops: plan, act, observe, verify, route, and resume across Hermes, OpenClaw, Codex, and MiniMax without hiding blockers or proof.
examples: Create a loop that watches tests and fixes failures until the suite passes
Show why Hermes or OpenClaw owns a mission lane and how the fused supervisor records proof

name: voice_accessibility_operator
description: Improve voice-first and accessibility-first operation for Fluxio: dictation recovery, keyboard navigation, screen-reader labels, plain-language confirmations, and low-typing workflows.
examples: Make the central composer usable with fewer typed corrections
Audit voice and keyboard control paths for accessibility blockers
